comments.body: Your code seems to be true.
Possible duplicate of How can I ask the Selenium-WebDriver to wait for few seconds in Java?

answers.body:
Put a wait or sleep before:

driver.findElement(By.xpath("//input[@value='Paypal']")).click();

and see if it can locate your element then, then it is just a timing issue.

or alternatively split your code:

WebElement element = driver.findElement(By.xpath("//input[@value='Paypal']"));
sleep(1000);
//always better to add a wait than a sleep. Sleep is only to test if it is the issue
element.click();

I suspect when you are trying to locate your element, it has not yet loaded.

Try this,

@Test
public void paypal() {
    Thread.sleep(3000);
    driver.findElement(By.xpath("//input[@value='Paypal']")).click();
    System.out.println("Current page title is : " + driver.getTitle() + ", time: " + System.currentTimeMillis());
}

you could use explicit wait: 

(new WebDriverWait(driver, 30)).withMessage("Waited too long for Paypal-Button").until(ExpectedConditions.presenceOfElementLocated(By.xpath("//input[@value='Paypal']")));
driver.findElement(By.xpath("//input[@value='Paypal']")).click();

title: Selenium: NoSuchElementException: Unable to locate element - method:xpath
